channel.title: Home on 3BIO-BioInfo Laboratory
channel.description: Recent content in Home on 3BIO-BioInfo Laboratory

item.title: Prediction of B-cell Epitopes for Vaccine Design
item.description: Our lab develops computational methods for predicting B-cell epitopes, which are crucial for rational vaccine design and antibody engineering. This research contributes to the development of more effective vaccines and therapeutic antibodies.

item.title: Prediction of Disease-Causing Variants in the Human Genome
item.description: We develop computational tools to predict the pathogenicity of genetic variants, helping to identify disease-causing mutations in the human genome. This research has important implications for personalized medicine and genetic counseling.

item.title: Rational Design of Modified Proteins
item.description: Our research focuses on the rational design of proteins with modified properties, utilizing computational methods to predict and optimize protein stability, solubility, and binding affinity. This work has applications in biotechnology, drug design, and industrial enzyme engineering.

item.title: Study of Dynamics of House Dust Mite Allergens from Family 5
item.description: Our research investigates the structural dynamics and biophysical properties of house dust mite allergens using computational approaches. This work contributes to understanding allergic mechanisms and developing better diagnostic and therapeutic strategies.

item.title: Synthetic Gene Circuits and Noise Control
item.description: We use stochastic modeling to study synthetic gene circuits and develop strategies for controlling biological noise. This research has applications in synthetic biology and understanding fundamental principles of gene regulation.

item.title: Opportunities
item.description: Thesis and Internship Positions We regularly offer opportunities for Master’s students, PhD candidates, and postdoctoral researchers interested in computational biology and bioinformatics. Available Positions Check back regularly for current openings, or contact us directly to inquire about opportunities. Requirements Strong background in computer science, bioinformatics, physics, or related fields Programming skills (Python, R, or similar) Interest in computational biology and biological problems Good communication skills in English How to Apply Please send your CV and a brief statement of interest to marianne.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Below is a list of our recent publications in computational biology, bioinformatics, and related fields.

item.title: Software
item.description: We develop and maintain several bioinformatics tools and software packages for the scientific community.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Courses Members of our laboratory teach various courses in bioinformatics, computational biology, and related fields at ULB. Course Information Information about specific courses will be added here. This includes: Undergraduate courses in bioinformatics Graduate courses in computational biology Specialized workshops and training sessions For Students If you’re interested in learning more about our teaching activities or taking courses with us, please check the ULB course catalog or contact us directly.

item.title: Team
item.description: Our team consists of faculty members, postdoctoral researchers, PhD students, and technical staff, all dedicated to advancing computational biology and bioinformatics research.
